item.title: A pretty minor detail.
item.content:encoded:
Okay, it may be a bit too nitpicky, but taking into consideration the sheer amount of effort the Foundation does to fool their relatively lower ranks to hide the ugly truth, I'd say locking the last part behind a clearance wall would be more acceptable in-universe.

Other than that, this collab has been extremely ejoyable and quite atmospheric. Like some had said, this feel like accidentally (or diabolically inspired) combo which turns out to be better than what it looks on paper.

Gave some seriously creepy vibes, and although we can infer what happened from the last entry, still gives an interesting premise and plot. I would suggest linking the last entry somewhere though.

+1

item.title: What is the cost of lies?
item.content:encoded:
"We burnt to the ground
Left a view to admire,
With buildings inside, church of white.
We burnt to the ground left a grave to admire."

Alliances forged by desperation and distrust have a habit of going wrong. This is the evidence of that failure. An accompanying piece to our GoI format, written by Modulum and I.

Credit to Rounderhouse for providing some last minute critique.

Image can be found here https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Pripyat#/media/File:Postýlky_v_mateřské_školce_-_panoramio.jpg and is CC-BY-SA 3.0.

This article is part of The Coldest War canon.
